memories: Walrus is a decentralized, verifiable blob storage platform built by Mysten Labs (the team behind Sui) that stores data as immutable blobs and binds every blob to a Sui object of type Blob.

Walrus encodes data using the RedStuff erasure-coding algorithm, an adaptation of the Twin-Code framework based on the RaptorQ fountain code, splitting each blob into primary and secondary slivers distributed across all shards in the system.

Walrus keeps storage overhead at about 4.5x the original blob size (roughly 5x expansion including metadata) instead of full replication, while still tolerating node failures and supporting efficient node recovery.

Walrus assumes more than 2/3 of shards are managed by honest storage nodes and tolerates up to 1/3 of shards being controlled by malicious or faulty storage nodes within each storage epoch; a blob is certified once 2/3 of storage-node receipt signatures are aggregated into an availability certificate.
Walrus storage epochs last 2 weeks on Mainnet and 1 day on Testnet, and a smart contract on the Sui blockchain controls how shards are assigned to storage nodes.

Walrus offers a fixed, USD-denominated storage cost of $0.023/GB/month, paid in WAL tokens whose required amount adjusts automatically as the WAL token price changes; SUI is paid separately for onchain gas fees.
Walrus uses the WAL token to pay for storage and to secure the network through staking, and relies on the Sui blockchain for payments, metadata management, resource management, and shard assignment.

Walrus enables efficient node recovery: with RedStuff a failed node can reconstruct its lost sliver by fetching only O(S/N) data (about the size of the lost part) instead of fetching and re-encoding the whole blob.

Walrus makes all uploaded blobs publicly available, so to keep data private users should encrypt it first with an encryption service like Seal; Walrus data is content-addressed and cryptographically verifiable, so you can prove it has not been altered.

Walrus is used via a CLI installed with Mysten Labs' suiup tool: users configure the client for Testnet or Mainnet, fund a Sui account, convert SUI to WAL, then run 'walrus store <file> --epochs N' to upload a blob identified by a blob ID.

Walrus charges a fixed per-blob metadata overhead of up to ~64 MB that dominates for blobs smaller than 10 MB, and Walrus Quilt is a batch storage tool that amortizes this metadata cost across many small blobs stored together.

Walrus availability can be proven cheaply: a light client proof that a BlobCertified event was emitted for a blob ID constitutes a proof of availability for the data without transmitting the full blob.
